title: Host->container provisioning seam: make provisioning data-driven
description:
One concept — provision a host artifact into the container — is shattered across 3 disjoint edit sites in 2 files / 2 languages.

FILES:
- run.sh:535-558 (env exports)
- run.sh:562-598 (compose template)
- run.sh:600-636 (sed-injected mounts)
- entrypoint.sh:43-75 (copy-and-chmod blocks)

Each artifact (ssh pubkey, claude dir, claude.json, gitconfig, pi dir, ssh-host, git-credentials) needs: (a) export var, (b) YAML mount line, (c) container-side 'if /tmp/host_X exists -> rm ~/.X; cp -r; chmod' block. Adding one config touches all three. SHALLOW module spread thin; no module owns what gets provisioned.

SOLUTION: Provisioning as data — a table of (host_path, container_tmp, dest, mode). Host emits mounts from the table; container copies from the same table.

BENEFIT: locality (one row per artifact), leverage (new config = new row), tests (mount-emitter becomes a pure fn: table -> YAML lines, assertable with no docker). Deletion test: kill the loop and 7 near-identical pairs reappear across 2 files -> earns its keep.
close_reason: Data-driven provisioning seam landed in 1c2889c; lib/provisioning.sh sourced by run.sh+entrypoint.sh; all 3 tests green

title: Deepen run.sh: carve testable seams from 490-line god-body
description:
run.sh:226-716 runs arg-parse -> git-detect -> identity -> worktree -> validate -> build -> export -> compose -> mount -> docker up -> ssh-attach all top-level on global vars. Ordering is implicitly load-bearing (worktree must precede WORKSPACE_DIR export). Only 1 test exists because nothing is callable in isolation.

PROBLEM: No seams. The whole launch flow is one procedural body; interface == implementation, untestable.

SOLUTION: Carve pure decisions out of I/O. parse_args, resolve_container_name, resolve_flavor, plan_worktree, render_compose become value-returning functions; docker/ssh/git side-effects kept at the edges only.

BENEFIT: The interface becomes the test surface — assert 'these args -> this config' with no docker. Umbrella issue; child issues #1/#2/#3 are concrete slices.

title: Provision container hook runtimes: bd, node20, python3, bun-symlink
description: Container copies host ~/.claude hooks; hooks fail because their runtimes are absent in the image. Curated fix (per user): provision runtimes that add value headless. Scan of all enabled hooks found needs: /usr/bin/bun (26, context-mode hardcodes path), python/python3 (13, security-guidance), node (1, context-mode-cache-heal.mjs), bd (1, beads). Add to Dockerfile/.go/.rust/.flutter: bd via official install.sh, python3+python-is-python3 apt, /usr/local/bin/bun->/usr/bin/bun symlink. Node 20 via NodeSource for base/go/rust (flutter already has node). Out of scope: gitkraken (removed), notify-send (desktop-only), claude installMethod cosmetic warning.
notes: den:rust rebuilt (HOST_UID=1000) and runtime-verified: bd/node20/bun//usr/bin/bun/python3/python all resolve, cargo+sccache intact, node runs .mjs. Image-level done. ACCEPTANCE PENDING: user confirms quiet SessionStart hooks in a live den:rust claude session.
close_reason: Hook runtimes (bd/node20/python3/bun symlink) provisioned in 2442234; user confirmed quiet SessionStart hooks in live den:rust session

title: Dockerfile flavors: collapse ~66 duplicated base lines into a shared base stage
description:
FILES: Dockerfile, Dockerfile.go, Dockerfile.rust, Dockerfile.flutter

The base setup (apt install, user creation, ssh/sudoers/tmux, entrypoint copy) is copy-pasted ~66 lines across 4 files. go/rust extend the debian base; flutter diverges on FROM (node:20). A security fix to the apt block must land in 4 files.

SOLUTION: Multi-stage shared base stage (den:base); flavors add only their toolchain via FROM den:base. Deepens 'the base image' into one definition.

HONEST CONSTRAINT: flutter's different FROM caps how much collapses — it will not fully fold in.

BENEFIT: locality (base changes once). Real seam — 3+ adapters already exist, not hypothetical. Independent of the run.sh epic.
notes: EVIDENCE (den-wa1, commit 2442234): runtime provisioning added fresh duplication confirming this issue. python3+python-is-python3 x4 files; /usr/bin/bun symlink x4; NodeSource node20 + bd install RUN block (~8 lines) x3 identical (base/go/rust). One logical change = 8 edits across 4 Dockerfiles. Flutter caveat confirmed: it took bd-only (node already present via FROM node:20), so a shared base stage will not fully fold flutter in. NOTE: image-level tool installs belong here, NOT in den-sj7.1 (whose table is host_path->container copy, a different seam).

title: Git-auth provisioning: single source of truth, de-triplicate GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL bootstrap
description:
FILES: entrypoint.sh:78-135 (3x copies), split with run.sh:321-336,526-533

The 3 auth methods (credentials file / GITHUB_TOKEN / identity) each re-implement the same ~10 lines: 'ensure GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL is set + echo export into .bashrc/.zshrc/.profile'. 20+ duplicated lines. No module owns the container's effective git config, and the user cannot tell which method won.

SOLUTION: Extract ensure_git_config_global() once. Treat git-auth setup as one deep module: small interface (env vars in), one observable outcome (effective config + a logged active method).

BENEFIT: locality (bootstrap in one place), kills 20 dup lines, leverage (a 4th method = one call, not a copy-paste block), becomes a testable seam: 'given these env vars -> git config global shows X'.

title: Compose assembly: replace heredoc + sed injection with data-driven render
description:
FILES: run.sh:560-636

Base compose YAML is a literal heredoc; 5 conditional mounts are spliced in via sed -i '/^    cap_add:/i\...'. The 'cap_add:' line is a load-bearing string marker — reorder the template and every mount breaks silently. Volume strings are shell-concatenated with no escaping.

SOLUTION: Build the volume list as an array; render compose once from data (deepen 'compose assembly' into a config -> yaml function). Removes sed entirely.

BENEFIT: tests (render is pure, golden-file testable), locality (mount decisions live together, not 5 scattered sed calls), removes the implicit marker-line coupling. Shares the mount-as-data idea with the provisioning-seam issue — that one is cross-seam, this one is host-side render.
